titleEn: EU–Vietnam poultry campaign marks AMR awareness drive
titleZh: 欧盟-越南家禽宣传活动推动抗菌素耐药性意识提升
summaryZh: 为纪念世界抗菌素耐药性意识周，欧盟联合资助了一项信息推广活动，强调越南在家禽生产中合理使用抗生素的进展，以及欧洲标准与当地市场期望的兼容性。活动展示了欧盟家禽生产中的严格生物安全、疫苗接种、日常健康监测等措施，以及越南自2026年起全面禁止预防性使用抗生素的路线图。文章突出了可追溯性、无残留肉品和消费者信任的重要性，但未涉及智慧畜牧技术。
featuredReason: 该新闻聚焦于家禽生产中抗生素管理的政策与标准，虽未直接涉及智慧畜牧技术，但为行业提供了关于食品安全和可持续生产的重要背景，适合关注法规趋势的从业者参考。
contentFull: Markets & policy Market trends Sustainability EU–Vietnam poultry campaign marks AMR awareness drive Producers highlight strict standards and traceable, residue-safe meat 18 November 2025 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media Asia To mark the World Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) Awareness Week, which begins today (18–24 November), the EU co-financed the 'European Poultry – From Our Farms to Your Tables' information and promotional campaign to highlight Vietnam's progress in the rational use of antibiotics in poultry production and the compatibility of European standards with local market expectations, according to a press release. In 2018, the Vietnamese government prohibited the use of antibiotic growth promoters in livestock feed. It also adopted a roadmap to phase out the preventive use of antibiotics across livestock production, with a full ban entering into force on 1 January 2026; therapeutic use remains allowed under veterinary prescription. These steps reflect a shared commitment by Vietnam and the European Union to high standards of animal health and food safety.The European Union bans the routine and preventive group use of antibiotics in food-producing animals. Antibiotics may be administered only on veterinary prescription, when medically justified to treat or control disease in animals. Treatment is carried out under strict veterinary supervision, observing the withdrawal period required to ensure that residues remain within the legal limits (MRLs). Prevention and welfare are a priority. In line with the Farm to Fork strategy, the EU aims to further reduce antimicrobial use while maintaining high food safety standards and full traceability.European poultry producers employ preventive measures such as strict on-farm biosecurity, vaccination programmes tailored to disease risk, daily flock-health monitoring, attention to water and feed quality, and comfortable housing conditions (ventilation, lighting, space). Medications remain a treatment tool — used as rarely as possible and only when medically justified - to protect animal health and welfare. This approach promotes flock health, reduces the risk of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and strengthens consumer trust.Thanks to these strict procedures, EU poultry meat is safe for consumers and does not contain harmful residues. This is ensured by EU and national laws that strictly control the use of veterinary medicines and the sale of meat.European poultry batches are fully traceable and comply with Vietnam's MRLs and are accompanied by full documentation of origin and testing. For importers, retail chains and the HORECA sector, this means a stable supply, consistent quality and compliance with local regulations – both now and in the future, as the rules become more stringent. Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all WOAH reports 2.16 million birds lost to HPAI in May Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 1 July 2026 1 minute read Canadian firms launch world's earliest in-ovo egg sexing system In-ovo sexing 1 July 2026 1 minute read Hyperspectral technology determines sex from day four of incubation Nepal culls birds as H5N1 outbreak spreads to 11 districts Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 1 July 2026 1 minute read Kathmandu Valley worst affected as zoo closes to public Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International
translationZh:
为纪念世界抗菌素耐药性意识周（11月18日至24日），欧盟联合资助了“欧洲家禽——从农场到餐桌”信息推广活动，旨在强调越南在家禽生产中合理使用抗生素的进展，以及欧洲标准与当地市场期望的兼容性，据一份新闻稿称。

2018年，越南政府禁止在牲畜饲料中使用抗生素生长促进剂。它还通过了一项路线图，逐步淘汰牲畜生产中抗生素的预防性使用，全面禁令将于2026年1月1日生效；治疗性使用在兽医处方下仍然允许。这些步骤反映了越南和欧盟对动物健康和食品安全高标准的共同承诺。

欧盟禁止在食用动物中常规和预防性群体使用抗生素。抗生素只能在兽医处方下，且在医学上合理用于治疗或控制动物疾病时方可使用。治疗在严格的兽医监督下进行，并遵守停药期，以确保残留物在法律限值（最大残留限量）之内。预防和福利是优先事项。根据“从农场到餐桌”战略，欧盟旨在进一步减少抗菌药物的使用，同时保持高食品安全标准和完全可追溯性。

欧洲家禽生产者采取预防措施，如严格的农场生物安全、针对疾病风险定制的疫苗接种计划、每日鸡群健康监测、关注水质和饲料质量，以及舒适的饲养条件（通风、照明、空间）。药物仍然是一种治疗工具——尽可能少用，且仅在医学上合理时使用——以保护动物健康和福利。这种方法促进鸡群健康，降低抗菌素耐药性风险，并增强消费者信任。

由于这些严格程序，欧盟禽肉对消费者安全，且不含有害残留物。欧盟和各国法律严格控制兽药的使用和肉类销售，确保了这一点。

欧洲家禽批次完全可追溯，符合越南的最大残留限量，并附带完整的来源和检测文件。对于进口商、零售连锁店和餐饮行业，这意味着稳定的供应、一致的质量以及符合当地法规——无论是现在还是未来，随着规则变得更加严格。
contentHtml:
EU–Vietnam poultry campaign marks AMR awareness drive 
Producers highlight strict standards and traceable, residue-safe meat
